title: 1.21.1 and 1.21.2 missing in quay.io repository
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**
Latest versions of buildah: `1.21.1` and `1.21.2` are not available on quay.io. We would like to check if we can use those because `1.21.0` doesn't pass vulnerabilities check in our company, while `latest` is ok. Unfortunately we can't import `latest` images into our internal registry, so we'd like to check fixed versions.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Open https://quay.io/repository/buildah/stable?tab=tags
2. Search for tag `1.21`

Only `1.21.0` is present

comments.body:
...done.  The automation for 'stable' is working as designed, though I did note that 'testing' is bringing in version `1.21.2`.  So as that version moves through the process, it will eventually show up in stable.

@lsm5 is anything abnormal blocking 1.21.2 moving out of F34 updates-testing (I'm just double-checking)?

>periodically grab the stable:latest image

Clarification: This won't get you a newer buildah version, but it may get you past the vulnerability scanning since the rest of the image's packages are continuously updated (daily).

Unfortunately I work in tightly controlled environment and can't import `stable:latest` 😢 
`1.21.0` has only one vulnerability that prevents us from import: https://github.com/frenzymadness/CVE-2021-3572
I am not against this.  I to agree that this is the version of Buildah not the version of the image.

Hi, just scanned `quay.io/buildah/testing:latest` (image digest: `f7c27ddf5efdd1b45006166ce590046b73aae2c9162fd100f0aa74d2f58fb531`).

Unfortunately it still has the same vulnerability.

> Unfortunately it still has the same vulnerability.

Thanks for checking, that's helpful to know, so my fear is a real possibility then.  The buildah package could update, get an image tagged, and still not update `pip` (or whatever has a security problem next).  Worse, since we never update the tagged images, those problems will persist no matter the buildah version.

One potential complication with continuously updating the tagged images is, it will force users to download new images every day, even if there are no package changes (since the timestamp is different) :frowning: Off-hand, what we could do is track two things when deciding to build: The buildah version, and a hash of the package listing (includes version numbers).  Then, only push updated version-tagged images if the package-list hash changes.

Note: This doesn't at all address actual packages getting updated due to security issues, that workflow needs to move forward on it's own.  The proposed change would simply mean we actually present those updates over time, despite the buildah version not changing.

@rostrovsky please give another poke at `quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.21.0`  My PR w/ updated workflow just merged.  If we're lucky, your CVE fix also made it in (I didn't check).  If not, you may need to hunt down the specific package maintainer(s) and see what's what. On our-end, we'll now build & pushing the latest version-tagged image on a daily basis.  So soon as any fix gets into the main repos, we'll pick it up at most 1-day later.

Shortly I'll make the same workflow change to podman and skpeo as well.

Hi, just to confirm: `quay.io/buildah/stable@sha256:d825ce3e14bbff0a4f05c2168fb786e7ba324607a26edd71f37e63bfe19f42e2` does not have any substantial vulnerabilities. We'll be able to import it. Thanks! 👍
